title: fraxinus angustifolia var. australis
intro: Fraxinus angustifolia var. australis, also known as the English or narrow-leaved blue ash, is a deciduous tree native to the Iberian Peninsula and northwestern Africa. The medicinal properties of Fraxinus angustifolia var. australis are attributed to its bark, which contains saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. This plant has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can lead to health benefits such as reduced pain and inflammation, as well as improved immune function. Fraxinus angustifolia var. australis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
